mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with 20d returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and consumer credit (RSAFS) signals robust supply-side capacity and demand dynamics, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1) or 60d returns underperform baseline by > 15% in 3-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets, prompting inflationary pressures that reduce equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 5, or UNRATE fails to correlate with 5d returns (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate elevated demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through sectoral spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns underperform baseline by > 10% in 3-year backtests or HOUST shows no significant correlation with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market performance, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns underperform baseline by > 10% in 3-year backtests or PAYEMS fails to correlate with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real personal consumption (RSAFS) below 360 billion signals weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings face downward pressure.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns outperform baseline by > 15% in 3-year backtests or RSAFS shows no significant correlation with returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) combined with low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2%) indicates efficient supply chains and stable pricing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows no significant association with SPX returns (p > 0.1) or 20d returns underperform baseline by > 10% in 3-year backtests.
